items.title: Elon Musk sparks controversy with gesture resembling Nazi salute | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>A hand gesture by billionaire Elon Musk during a speech at Donald Trump's inauguration is causing controversy. At a celebratory rally in Washington, while thanking the crowd, the world's richest man slapped his right hand to his chest and extended his arm outward in a gesture that some say resembled a Nazi salute. In an apparent response to the controversy, Elon Musk posted on his X platform that his opponents needed - quote "better dirty tricks". 

#elonmusk #trump #hitler

items.title: President Trump signs first round of executive orders, withdraws from Paris agreement | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Donald Trump is once again president of the United States. He took the oath of office and began his second term as the 47th president of the United States, promising what he called a 'new golden age for America.' At a rally of his supporters packed inside a Washington DC stadium, Trump wasted no time and began signing a series of executive orders.  One of the first was to withdraw the United States from the Paris climate treaty. 

For some analysis of Trump's first moves in office and their implications we spoke to DW correspondent Max Zander in Washington D.C., Brian Murray, Director of the Nicholas Institute for Energy, Environment & Sustainability at Duke University, and Ethan Bearman, an attorney and adjunct professor at Loyola Law School Los Angeles.

00:00 Intro & report
04:10 Interview Max Zander
09:43 Interview Brian Murray, Duke University
14:45 Interview Ethan Bearman,  Loyola Law School
19:57 Reactions 

#donaldtrump #unitedstates #parisclimateagreement

items.title: Trump's shifting timeline for peace in Ukraine | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Anyone listening for a mention of Ukraine in President Trump's inaugural speech was disappointed. During his reelection campaign, Trump vowed to strike a peace deal to end Russia's war in Ukraine on his first day back in the White House. But that ambitious promise has since been scaled back - with Trump now speaking about bringing peace to Ukraine within six months. 

0:00 Trump talking about his timeline for peace in Ukraine
1:00 Rachel Rizzo, Senior Fellow, The Atlantic Council

#russiaukrainewar #donaldtrump #peacedeal

items.title: Donald Trump sworn in for second presidency | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Donald Trump today took the oath of office and began his second term as president of the United States, promising a new golden age for America. He becomes only the second person to return to the White House after losing an election. The ceremony was held inside the US Capitol, where Trump supporters rampaged following his defeat four years ago. This time round Trump got straight down to business and signed a series of executive orders.

00:00 Donald Trump takes the Oath of office for his second term of presidency of the USA
03:14 Ines Pohl, DW Washinfton bureau chief
08:29 Boris Vormann, Professor of Politics, Bard College Berlin
18:29 Benjamin Alvarez Gruber, DW US Correspondent

items.title: Live: Donald Trump inaugurated as 47th US president | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Donald Trump is assuming office on 20 January to become 47th president of the United States. The inauguration ceremony marks the commencement of Trump's second, non-consecutive term as president. Traditionally, the president-elect and vice-president-elect start the events by arriving at the White House and attending a brief meeting. The outgoing president then accompanies them to to the Capitol for the swearing-in ceremony. The inauguration will be moved indoors due to freezing temperatures. The address and the speeches will take place inside the US Capitol's rotunda.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Both Israel and Hamas have fulfilled their commitments on day one of the Gaza ceasefire deal. Three Israeli hostages taken captive by Hamas in the October 7 terror attacks have been freed in return for 90 Palestinians held in Israeli jails.

items.title: Palestinians return to their destroyed neighborhoods in Gaza | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Palestinians are beginning to return to their neighborhoods in Gaza after the ceasefire between Israel and Hamas came into force. But much of the territory lies in ruins. Jabaliya refugee camp in the north of Gaza is one area that's been completely destroyed. It used to house tens of thousands of people.

items.title: Russia's stealth oil ships: How Moscow dodges sanctions | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Can anyone stop Russia's shadow-fleet -- the rag-tag collection of ships quietly moving oil around the world in spite of Western sanctions.  The US and UK are trying -- they've now blacklisted 183 vessels that they say belong to the fleet. But will that work? And could it finally start sapping Moscow's oil revenues, with which it funds its war with Ukraine?

items.title: What the world can expect in Trump's second term | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Donald Trump is just hours away from being sworn-in as the next president of the United States. On the eve of his inauguration, he held a campaign-style rally in Washington - where he repeated his pledge to begin mass deportations of undocumented migrants as soon as he's back in power. Shortly before the rally, he participated in a wreath-laying ceremony at Arlington National Cemetery honouring fallen members of the US military.
The effects of a second Trump presidency extend across the globe and very much here in Germany. His planned tariffs on European goods will impact the German economy, and his stance on NATO members' defense spending will impact German security. As Germany prepares to go to the polls on the 23rd of February, the Trump effect is front of mind among German leaders.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Aid flows into Gaza as people return to devastated homes | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Both Israel and Hamas have fulfilled their commitments on day one of the Gaza ceasefire deal. Three Israeli hostages taken captive by Hamas in the October 7th terror attacks have been freed in return for dozens of Palestinians held in Israeli jails. Meanwhile, aid deliveries to Gaza are being stepped up as tens of thousands of Gazans return to cities devastated by 15 months of war.

00:00 Cerasefire holds through first day
00:27 DW's Tania KrĂ¤mer reports from Jerusalem
06:42 DW speaks with Amjad Shawa, head of the Palestinian Non Governmental Organizations Network from Deir Al Balah in the central Gaza Strip
